title: ginkgoaceae
intro: The Ginkgoaceae rank is a family of plants, specifically a group of flowering plants that are closely related and share similar characteristics. This family is characterized by having simple, fan-shaped leaves and producing small, inconspicuous flowers. The Ginkgoaceae family is relatively small, consisting of only one living species, Ginkgo biloba, often referred to as the maidenhair tree. Native to China, the Ginkgoaceae family is one of the oldest living tree families, with fossil records dating back to the Early Cretaceous period, around 170 million years ago.
